title: Expression and purification of ATP13A4 from Saccharomyces cerevisiae
author: Sarah van Veen

steps.instruction: Plate the yeast from the glycerol stock on a YPD-agar plate and incubate for 48:00:00 at 30 °C . We used the Saccharomyces cerevisiae BY4743; MATa/MATa strain (his3D1/his3D1, leu2D0/leu2D0, LYS2/lys2D0, met15D0/MET15; ura3D0/ura3D0, YPL217c/YPL217c::kanMX4; a gift from J. Winderickx).
Inoculate 20 mL YPD medium from the fresh plate and incubate Overnight at 30 °C , 230 rpm, °C, in a shaking incubator.
Measure the OD of the overnight culture at 600 nm (OD 600 ). 1 OD 600 equals approximately 10 7 cells/ml.
Dilute the culture to 0.1 OD 600 in 10 mL YPD and incubate for 4-6 h at 30 °C , 230 rpm, °C, in a shaking incubator until the OD 600 of the culture lies between 0.4 and 0.8.
Spin down the cell amount equivalent to 2 OD 600 (= ± 2 x 10 7 cells) in swinging buckets ( 700 x g, °C, , 00:05:00 ).
Wash the pellet in 1 mL sterile milliQ water and resuspend with a pipet tip. Transfer to an Eppendorf tube.
Spin down the cells ( 2.500 x g, °C, ; 00:02:00 ) using a microcentrifuge.
Resuspend pellet in 1 mL 1x TE/LiAc.
Spin down the cells ( 2.500 x g, °C, ; 00:02:00 ) using a microcentrifuge.
Resuspend pellet in 100 µL 1x TE/LiAc.
Add 10 µL of single-stranded herring sperm DNA. To make single-stranded DNA, herring sperm DNA is boiled for 20 min in water bath and then immediately cooled on ice.
Add 1 to 5 µg of plasmid DNA. Vortex. We used the pYSG-IBA162 vector containing a yeast codon-optimized version of human ATP13A4 with C-terminal Twin-Strep tag (pYSG-IBA162-FLAG-hATP13A4-TwinStrep)
Add 600 µL of PEG solution. Vortex.
Incubate for 00:45:00 at 30 °C (volume = approximately 730 µl).
Add DMSO to a final concentration of 10% (approximately 73 µl).
Heat shock at 42 °C for 00:15:00 .
Cool on ice for 1-2 min.
Spin down the cells ( 2.500 x g, °C, ; 00:02:00 ) using a microcentrifuge.
Resuspend pellet in 200 µL of YPD and incubate for 02:00:00 at 30 °C .
Spin down the cells ( 2.500 x g, °C, ; 00:02:00 ) using a microcentrifuge.
Resuspend the cell pellet in sterile milliQ water and plate onto an MM-uracil agar plate.
Incubate the plate for 48:00:00 at 30 °C to recover transformants.
Select colonies and grow them in 20 mL MM-Ura medium at 28 °C and 175 rpm, °C, for 24:00:00 .
Collect yeast cell pellets via centrifugation ( 1000 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes ) and resuspend in 4 L of MM-Leu medium. Induce ATP13A4 expression with 1 millimolar (mM) CuSO₄ and grow for 24:00:00 .
Measure OD 600 of the second pre-culture and inoculate 4 L of MM-Ura medium to a final OD 600 of 0.05. Grow Overnight at 28 °C and 175 rpm, °C, .
Collect yeast cell pellets via centrifugation ( 1000 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes ) and weigh the pellet. The cell pellet can be used immediately or stored at -80°C.
If the yeast cell pellet was frozen, thaw it in a water bath at 37 °C .
Fill the chamber with 200 mL yeast suspension (add TES in buffer if not enough) and add 200 mL cold glass beads. Chamber should be as full as possible.
Break the yeast cells using a BeadBeater.
Resuspend the pellet in TESin buffer (volume (ml) = approximately 1 x the weight of the cell pellet (g) as determined in go to step #27 ).
Spin down the cells ( 1.000 x g, 4 °C, ; 00:10:00 ).

Resuspend cell pellet in TEKS buffer (volume (ml) = approximately 2 x the weight of the cell pellet (g) as determined in go to step #27 ) and incubate for 00:15:00 at 4 °C while mixing with a magnetic stirrer.
Recover crude extract using Büchner funnel and vacuum pump. Test the pH of the lysate by using pH paper and, if necessary, adjust the pH of the crude extract to pH 7.5 with saturated NaOH solution.
Bead-beat in cold room for 5 min. Pause for 3 min after every min to reduce heating of the sample and BeadBeater.
Fill ice water jacket with crushed ice and water.
Centrifuge the resulting supernatant at 200.000 x g, 4 °C, for 01:00:00 .
Discard the resulting pellet and centrifuge the resulting supernatant at 20.000 x g, 4 °C, for 00:20:00 .
Centrifuge the crude extract at 2.000 x g, 4 °C, for 00:20:00 .

Resuspend the resulting pellet (that is, the light membrane fraction, P3) in HS buffer. The volume for resuspension should be determined by eye and be the minimal amount required to homogeneously dissolve the pellet.

Resuspend the resulting pellet (that is, the heavy membrane fraction, P2) in HS buffer. The volume for resuspension should be determined by eye and be the minimal amount required to homogeneously dissolve the pellet.
Measure OD 600 of the pre-culture and inoculate 200 mL MM-Ura medium to a final OD 600 of 0.2 and grow for 12:00:00 at 28 °C and 175 rpm, °C, .

Determine the protein concentration of the membrane fractions using a classical Bradford assay (with known BSA concentrations as a protein standard). The measured protein concentration typically ranges from 20 to 50 mg/mL.
Aliquot the membrane fractions, flash-freeze in liquid N 2 and store at -80 °C. The membranes can be stored at -80 °C for a maximum of 6 months.
Solubilize the membranes by stirring with a magnetic stirrer for 01:00:00 at 4 °C .
Centrifuge at 200.000 x g, 4 °C, for 01:00:00 to remove insoluble material.
Incubate the supernatant with Strep-Tactin®XT resin for 03:00:00 at 4 °C . 50 ml falcon tubes and a head-over-head rotator can be used for this step.
Wash resin 5 times with 20 column volumes of SSR buffer.
Elute ATP13A4 with SSR buffer supplemented with 50 mM biotin (E1-5).
Analyze purification quality using SDS-PAGE followed by Coomassie staining or immunoblotting.
Estimate protein concentration by running an SDS-PAGE gel with BSA standards and staining with Coomassie dye. Protein quantification by SDS-PAGE and Coomassie staining by Sarah van Veen
Dilute membranes to 5 mg/ml in SSR buffer supplemented with 5 mg/ml DDM.

Equilibrate Strep-Tactin®XT resin by washing it with SSR buffer (10 column volumes; 300 x g, 4 °C, , 00:03:00 ). Calculate how much resin is required: 0.5 mL beads (1 ml slurry) for 400 mg total protein. Total volume of resin = 1 column volume
Elution 1: add 600 µl elution buffer and collect the eluate (E1).
Pour the mixture into a large column and allow it to drip through by gravity flow.
Elution 2: add 1600 µl elution buffer, incubate for 5 min in head-over-head rotator at 4 °C and collect the eluate (E2).
Elution 3: add 800 µl elution buffer, incubate for 5 min in head-over-head rotator at 4 °C and collect the eluate (E3).
Elution 4: add 800 µl elution buffer, incubate for 5 min in head-over-head rotator at 4 °C and collect the eluate (E4).

Elution 5: add 800 µl elution buffer, incubate for 5 min in head-over-head rotator at 4 °C and collect the eluate (E5). E2-5 can be collected together in 1 tube, as 1 elution fraction. E1 generally does not contain protein. Aliquot elution fractions before flash-freezing and store at -80°C.
